titleStmt.title: SII 26.301: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 301. (A.R. No. 286 of 1909.) TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: This inscription records a royal order drafted by tirumandira-ōlai Mīṉavaṇ-mūvēndavēḷāṉ and issued through his officers, to the dēvakaṉmi, śrīmāhēśvarakaṅgāṉi-cheyvār and śrīkāryam-cheyvār of the temple of Tiru[vai]yār-uḍaiyār of kuttaṉpākkam in Māṅgāḍu-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam granting the lands of 25 vēli from Pāripākkam, 40 vēli from Arumpākkam and 10 vēli from Māttūr, all belonging to the same nāḍu ḍēvadāṉa-iṟaiyili for the offerings to this god. This document was attested by Toṇḍaimāṉ, Nigariliśōḻa-pallavarayaṉ, Villavarayaṉ, Kaliṅgarāyaṉ and Mūvēndarayaṉ, who figure as signatories.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī tribhuvanaccakravatti koneriṉmai koṇṭān jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa maṇṭalattu puliyūrkoṭṭamāṉa
kulottuṅka coḻavaḷanāṭṭu māṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu kūttaṉ pākkattu Uṭaiyār tiru vaiyār Uṭaiyār koyilil tevaka
ṉmikkum śrīmāheśvara kaṅkāṇi ceyvārkaḷukkum śrīkāriyañ ceyvāṉukkum It(ta)tevarku veṇṭu nivaṉtaṅkaḷukku Iṟuppa
tāka Innāṭṭu pāripākkam pottakappaṭi nilam Irupattai veliyum Arumpākkam nilam nāṟpatiṟṟu veliyum māttūr nilam
patiṟṟuveliyum Ittevaṟkku tevatāna Iṟaiyiliyāka Iṭakkaṭavatākap peṟa veṇumeṉṟu vaṟcasarāyarum namakkuc collivarakā
ṭṭi Ippaṭi namakku toṇṭaimānum namakkuc coṉṉavāṟe Ippaṭi ceyyakkaṭavatāka colli kaṇakkiṉum Iṭṭuk koḷḷakkaṭa
varkaḷ varikkūṟu ceyvārkaḷukkuc coṉnom Iṉnilam Irupattoṉṟāvatiṉ Etirāmāṇṭu mutal Ittevarku tevatāṉa Iṟaiyiliyākak
kaik koṇṭu nivaṉtañ celuttap paṇṇuka Eḻutiṉāṉ tirumantira Olai mīṉavaṉ muveṉtaveḷāṉ yāṇṭu Irupattoṉṟāvatu 2 10 1 (1/2)vatu nā
ḷ 2 100 5 śva Ivai toṇṭaimāṉ Eḻuttu Ivai nikarilicoḻa pallavarayaṉeḻuttu Ivai villavarāyaṉ Eḻuttu Ivaik kaliṅkarāyaṉ Eḻuttu
Ivai varakuṇarāyaṉ Eḻuttu Ivai muveṉtarayaṉeḻuttu
Digital edition of SII 26.301 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
